name: writeFile
description: Write content to a file. Use with caution; overwrites existing files.
intent: create or overwrite whole files with literal content
examples: Create a new hello.txt containing Hello, world!
Overwrite the README with new markdown content
whenToUse: The user wants to write a complete file in one shot.
The entire desired content is known up front.
Creating a new file or fully replacing an existing file is acceptable.
whenNotToUse: Only a small snippet inside an existing file needs to change. Use editFile instead.
The change is a regex replacement across many files. Use editFile with isRegex=true instead.
The user only wants to read the file. Use readFile instead.
